summary: S680 (Smith/McKeon) requires new AI data centers and crypto mining facilities to source 100% of electricity from new Class I renewables (solar, wind, hydro, geothermal) or new nuclear. Facilities must submit energy plans to BPU for certification. Senate Environment and Energy Committee reported favorably 3-2 on March 16, 2026. Now referred to Senate Budget and Appropriations Committee. NJBIA opposed, calling mandate impractical. Key caveat: requirements would not take effect until most other PJM states adopt similar measures. Companion bill A4710 in Assembly.
bill_name: S680 - Data Center Clean Energy Mandate

summary: Residents and local officials held a town hall in January 2026 to discuss concerns about a proposed data center and its impact on local resources. West Deptford is among the 27+ NJ municipalities hosting operational or planned data centers. Community opposition focused on environmental impacts, energy consumption, and water usage. Specific project details not yet public.

Warren County Commissioner James R. Kern III (also incoming NJAC president) distributed a model data center siting ordinance to all mayors and governing bodies in Warren County. The advisory document is designed to help municipalities evaluate large-scale data center proposals against master plans, zoning objectives, and community priorities. Covers impacts on infrastructure, environment, and community character. Follows Phillipsburg's data center ban in the same county.

Assembly Bill 6181 would prohibit applicants for data center development approvals under the Municipal Land Use Law from entering non-disclosure agreements with approving authorities or municipal agencies that would conceal project details or limit public review. Aims to increase transparency in local data center siting decisions.
